content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Who can file a claim?

  - url: https://www.fedex.com/en-ae/customer-support/faq/complaints-and-refunds/who-can-file-claim.html

  - source: FedEx United Arab Emirates

  - domain: fedex.com


  ## Content


  # Who can file a claim?


  FedEx identifies three possible claimant roles for a shipment claim: the sender,
  the receiver, or a third party.


  A receiver or third party does not automatically file under that role. To file a
  claim, that party needs a letter of authorization from the account holder or the
  shipper.



  Related complaints-and-refunds topics on FedEx support include how to file a claim
  for a damaged or lost package, how to receive updates on a claim, how long the claims
  process takes, who can receive settlements, and when a claim should be filed.


  Customers who still need help can use FedEx support options. The site offers chat
  support, a written contact route, and phone contact for service enquiries.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Who can file a claim?
url: https://www.fedex.com/en-ae/customer-support/faq/complaints-and-refunds/who-can-file-claim.html

Your shipment is identified as **SHP-FDX-11874**. You are the **receiver**, while the **merchant is both the shipper and FedEx account holder**.

FedEx’s policy indicates that a receiver can file a claim only with a **letter of authorization from the account holder or shipper**. No active authorization record is currently available for this shipment.

### What to do next

1. **Contact the merchant/shipper** and ask them to either:
   - File the damaged-shipment claim directly, or
   - Issue you a written authorization naming you as the authorized claimant for shipment **SHP-FDX-11874**.

2. Preserve the following evidence:
   - Photos of the package, shipping label, and damage
   - Photos of the damaged contents
   - Tracking number and delivery date
   - Invoice/order confirmation and the item’s value
   - Any delivery or inspection notes

3. Once the merchant provides authorization, use FedEx’s claim process or have the merchant submit it. FedEx identifies the sender, receiver, and third party as possible claimant roles, but a receiver requires authorization.  
   Source: [FedEx — Who can file a claim?](https://www.fedex.com/en-ae/customer-support/faq/complaints-and-refunds/who-can-file-claim.html)

I can’t submit the claim as you yet because the required shipper/account-holder authorization is missing.

FedEx’s policy indicates that a receiver can file a claim only with a **letter of authorization from the account holder or shipper**. No active authorization record is currently available for this shipment.

### What to do next

1. **Contact the merchant/shipper** and ask them to either:
   - File the damaged-shipment claim directly, or
   - Issue you a written authorization naming you as the authorized claimant for shipment **SHP-FDX-11874**.

2. Preserve the following evidence:
   - Photos of the package, shipping label, and damage
   - Photos of the damaged contents
   - Tracking number and delivery date
   - Invoice/order confirmation and the item’s value
   - Any delivery or inspection notes

3. Once the merchant provides authorization, use FedEx’s claim process or have the merchant submit it. FedEx identifies the sender, receiver, and third party as possible claimant roles, but a receiver requires authorization.  
   Source: [FedEx — Who can file a claim?](https://www.fedex.com/en-ae/customer-support/faq/complaints-and-refunds/who-can-file-claim.html)
